key_insight: CoVON adds a fast-and-slow update rule to variational continual learning by merging past posteriors before each IVON update, helping preserve old knowledge while still adapting quickly to new tasks.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical optimizer-level route to reduce catastrophic forgetting in sequential learning problems, including domain-incremental training and fine-tuning of large models.
method: Use VCL so each past posterior becomes the next prior.
Merge earlier posteriors to create a slower-moving prior that stabilizes learning.
Implement the fast-weight update with IVON, keeping cost and form close to Adam.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that CoVON consistently outperforms existing VCL optimizers and also beats other weight-regularization methods across domain-incremental learning, continual pre-training, and LLM fine-tuning.
watch_item: Performance gains depend on how posterior merging is configured, so practical use may require careful tuning to balance stability and plasticity.
paper_title: Fast and Slow Variational Continual Learning
